title: Social Media at Work: Help or Hindrance?
subtitle: 工作中的社交媒体是助力还是分心

paragraphs.en: Social media can be a powerful communication tool for employees, helping them to collaborate, share ideas and solve problems. Research has shown that 82% of employees think social media can improve work relationships and 60% believe it can support decision-making processes. These beliefs contribute to a majority of workers connecting with colleagues on social media, even during work hours.
paragraphs.zh: 社交媒体可以成为员工强大的沟通工具，帮助他们协作、分享想法和解决问题。研究显示，82%的员工认为社交媒体能改善工作关系，60%认为它能支持决策过程。这些观念促使大多数员工都在社交媒体上与同事保持联系，甚至在工作时间也是如此。

paragraphs.en: Employers typically worry that social media is a productivity killer; more than half of U.S. employers reportedly block access to social media at work. In my research with 277 employees of a healthcare organization I found these concerns to be misguided. Social media doesn't reduce productivity nearly as much as it kills employee retention.
paragraphs.zh: 雇主通常担心社交媒体是生产力杀手；据报道，超过一半的美国雇主在工作中禁止使用社交媒体。在我对一家医疗机构277名员工的研究中，我发现这些担忧是被误导的。社交媒体对生产力的影响远不如对员工留存率的影响大。

paragraphs.en: In the first part of the study I surveyed the employees about why and how they used platforms like Facebook, Twitter, or LinkedIn. Respondents were then asked about their work behaviors, including whether they felt motivated in their jobs and showed initiative at work. I found employees who engage in online social interactions with co-workers through social media blogs tend to be more motivated and come up with innovative ideas.
paragraphs.zh: 在研究的第一部分，我调查了员工使用Facebook、Twitter或LinkedIn等平台的原因和方式，然后询问他们的工作行为，包括是否有工作动力和主动性。我发现，通过社交媒体与同事进行在线互动的员工更有动力，也更容易提出创新想法。

paragraphs.en: But when employees interact with individuals outside the organization, they are less motivated and show less initiative. In the second part of the study I found 76% of employees using social media for work took an interest in other organizations they found on social media. When I examined how respondents expressed openness to new careers and employers, I found that they engaged in some key activities including researching new organizations and making new work connections.
paragraphs.zh: 但当员工与组织外部的人互动时，动力和主动性反而降低。在研究的第二部分，我发现76%使用社交媒体工作的员工对在社交媒体上发现的其他组织产生了兴趣。当我研究受访者如何表达对新职业和雇主的开放态度时，发现他们会研究新组织并建立新的工作人脉。

paragraphs.en: These findings present a dilemma for managers: employees using social media at work are more engaged and more productive, but they are also more likely to leave your company. Managers should implement solutions that neutralize the retention risk caused by social media. They can create social media groups in which employees will be more likely to collaborate and less likely to share withdrawal intentions or discussions about external job opportunities. Managers can also use social media to directly reduce turnover intentions, by recognizing employees' accomplishments and giving visibility to employees' success stories.
paragraphs.zh: 这给管理者带来了两难境地：在工作中使用社交媒体的员工更投入、更高效，但也更可能离职。管理者应采取措施化解社交媒体带来的留存风险，比如创建内部社交媒体群组，让员工更多协作而非分享离职意向或讨论外部机会。管理者还可以通过社交媒体认可员工成就、展示员工成功故事，直接降低跳槽意向。
